# Benchmarks

The `test/benchmarks/` directory holds end-to-end validation benchmarks for
`YelmoModel`. Each benchmark exercises a specific solver or pipeline
component against either a **closed-form analytical solution** or a
**committed YelmoMirror reference fixture** generated by the Fortran backend.

Fixtures under `test/benchmarks/fixtures/` are committed to the repository.
CI loads them directly; `test/benchmarks/regenerate.jl` rebuilds them locally
when you need to refresh after a physics change.

## Benchmark summary

| Benchmark | Solver(s) tested | Validation target | Test file(s) |
|---|---|---|---|
| [BUELER-B Halfar dome](bueler-b.md) | SIA | Analytical Halfar / Bueler 2005 | `test_sia.jl` |
| [EISMINT-1 Moving](eismint-moving.md) | SIA + adaptive PC | Huybrechts 1996 + YelmoMirror lockstep | `test_eismint_moving.jl`, `test_eismint_moving_lockstep.jl` |
| [ISMIP-HOM-C](ismip-hom-c.md) | SSA | 180° rotational symmetry | `test_hom_c.jl` |
| [MISMIP3D Standard](mismip3d.md) | SSA (+ ATT ramp, adaptive PC, implicit advection) | YelmoMirror lockstep | `test_mismip3d_stnd.jl`, `test_mismip3d_stnd_lockstep.jl`, `test_mismip3d_stnd_att.jl`, `test_mismip3d_stnd_implicit.jl` |
| [Trough F17](trough.md) | SSA / DIVA | YelmoMirror fixture | `test_trough.jl`, `test_trough_diva.jl` |
| [CalvingMIP Exp 1](calvingmip.md) | SSA + level-set calving hook | YelmoMirror fixture | `test_calvingmip_exp1.jl` |
| [Slab advection stability](slab-advection.md) | Advection kernel only | Unconditional stability of implicit scheme | `test_slab_advection.jl` |
| [Adaptive predictor-corrector](adaptive-dt.md) | Adaptive timestepping (HEUN, FE-SBE, AB-SAM) | Snapshot/restore round-trip + MISMIP3D comparison | `test_adaptive_dt.jl` |

## How to run

All benchmark test files activate the `test/` project and are runnable as
standalone scripts:

```bash
julia --project=test test/benchmarks/test_sia.jl
julia --project=test test/benchmarks/test_eismint_moving.jl
julia --project=test test/benchmarks/test_hom_c.jl
julia --project=test test/benchmarks/test_mismip3d_stnd.jl
julia --project=test test/benchmarks/test_mismip3d_stnd_lockstep.jl
julia --project=test test/benchmarks/test_mismip3d_stnd_att.jl
julia --project=test test/benchmarks/test_mismip3d_stnd_implicit.jl
julia --project=test test/benchmarks/test_trough.jl
julia --project=test test/benchmarks/test_trough_diva.jl
julia --project=test test/benchmarks/test_calvingmip_exp1.jl
julia --project=test test/benchmarks/test_slab_advection.jl
julia --project=test test/benchmarks/test_adaptive_dt.jl
```

The EISMINT-1 lockstep test, trough, and CalvingMIP regression tests require
their committed YelmoMirror fixtures to be present in
`test/benchmarks/fixtures/`. These are checked in and do not require a Fortran
build to run against.  To regenerate a fixture from scratch (requires a compiled
`libyelmo_c_api.so`):

```bash
julia --project=test test/benchmarks/regenerate.jl <name> --overwrite
# e.g.: julia --project=test test/benchmarks/regenerate.jl trough_f17 --overwrite
```

## Benchmark infrastructure

Two backend patterns are used depending on whether a closed-form solution
exists:

**`AbstractBenchmark` (analytical)** — a struct carrying the benchmark
parameters implements `state(b, t)` (returning a `NamedTuple` of schema-keyed
fields) and `analytical_velocity(b, t)`. A generic `YelmoModel(b, t; ...)` 
constructor builds a model directly from the analytical state with no NetCDF
round-trip. Used by BUELER-B, EISMINT-1 Moving, ISMIP-HOM-C, MISMIP3D, and
CalvingMIP.

**`BenchmarkSpec` (YelmoMirror-generated fixtures)** — a `BenchmarkSpec` carries
a Yelmo Fortran namelist, synthetic-grid axes, an `end_time`, and an
`setup_initial_state!` callback. `run_mirror_benchmark!(spec)` drives
`YelmoMirror` and writes a NetCDF restart at each output time. `load_fixture`
constructs a `YelmoModel` from the resulting file. Used by EISMINT-1 Moving
(lockstep fixture), Trough F17, and CalvingMIP Exp 1.

See `test/benchmarks/README.md` for the full infrastructure description and
instructions for adding new benchmarks.
